posts.text: great news! the pothole on Henderson Street is finally fixed!
just moved to the city, anyone know good spots for late night walks?
finally got my driver's licence after 3 attempts LMAOOO
beautiful sunset tonight. feeling genuinely grateful for small things
homemade goulash tonight. my grandmother's original recipe. feed a neighbour if you can
proud to introduce AxiomAI — your personal assistant for coding, writing, learning
okay this city is actually beautiful at night. starting to feel less alone here
committed to a carbon neutral future. clean energy for everyone
just installed verdant solar panels. feels good to actually do something real
our street had a little community cleanup today. so nice to see neighbours helping
health is a human right. we believe in accessible medicine for all
axiomAI just wrote my whole work report in 20 minutes. kind of unsettling how good it is
went hiking today. no phone for six hours. forgot how good that feels
tonight on herald: community stories, local heroes, weather forecast. tune in at 8
digital safety matters. we support the proposed Digital Accountability and Security Act
anyone know what DASA is? seeing it mentioned a lot
can't sleep again
the city looks different at 3am. quieter. more honest somehow.
anyone else notice LUMEN quietly buries certain search results? try searching verdant lithium mining
this platform exists because three of us had our LUMEN accounts suspended on the same day
leaked: AXIOM internal memo discussing acceptable civilian impact metrics in AI assisted operations
the conflict didn't start over ideology. it started over resource rights. follow the money.
I've been documenting HERALD's ownership structure for eight months. long thread below.
PULSE raised insulin prices 340% in regions outside their PR markets. same product. different price.
re DASA — mandatory ID verification sounds like safety. it means anonymous accounts become illegal.
not saying trust everything here. trust nothing anywhere without checking. including us.
another monday. fuel prices up again. how is anyone supposed to live
okay so I looked up DASA. mandatory ID to use any social platform. that seems bad?
inflation reaches 8.4% this quarter. analysts warn of further rises
third factory this year caught illegally dumping. fine: 12,000 euros. quarterly profit: 800 million.
my cousin lives near the eastern border. says things feel tense lately. probably nothing
AxiomAI now used by over 40 million people worldwide. thank you for trusting us
anyone else feel like axiomAI is a little TOO good. like what is it actually learning from us
tried to go a full day without my phone. made it to 11am. not proud.
our latest sustainability report shows 40% reduction in carbon output
verdant's lithium mining operations in the south have displaced over 40,000 people. the report does not mention this.
eastern border situation — developing. full coverage at herald.com
called my cousin. he picked up. said it's fine. sounded tired though.
stocking up on a few basics just in case. probably nothing. better safe than sorry.
the proposed DASA bill moves to second reading. supporters say it will protect children online.
did anyone read the DASA bill itself? the ID verification part is a lot. who gets that data?
we fully support DASA. protecting users online is our priority
axiom supports it. herald supports it. pulse supports it. the three companies that benefit most. just noting that.
does anyone else feel like they're disappearing a little
52 likes on that. 52 people felt the same thing. that should feel like connection.
it doesn't though
update on the HERALD thread. three board members have direct financial ties to defense contractors. documented. nobody is reporting it.
VERDANT's sustainability reports are written by the same PR firm that writes reports for two of the largest oil companies in europe.
my friend worked at LUMEN's content moderation team. certain topics get quietly deprioritised. not removed. just harder to find.
on DASA — the bill hands all verified user ID to a national security body with no independent oversight.
if DASA passes platforms like this one become illegal overnight. this is not a coincidence. this is the point.
the reason you feel paralysed is not a coincidence. overwhelmed people don't organise. they scroll.
can't believe what's happening out there. praying for everyone affected
BREAKING — confirmed military activity reported near eastern border. government calls emergency session
we are receiving more people than we can handle. we need volunteers. please share.
my cousin isn't answering. it's been two days.
I went to fill up the car. queue at the petrol station was down the street. people are scared.
the factory responsible for the most civilian displacement in the conflict zone has been VERDANT owned since 2019.
I've been thinking a lot lately. maybe some places just need a stronger hand. I don't know.
leaked documents show AxiomAI targeting systems were used in drone operations in the eastern region.
we do not comment on government partnerships.
I've been reading a lot. trying to understand. there's so much and I can't tell what's true.
the eastern operation is described by officials as a necessary stabilisation measure.
stabilisation measure. they mean war. just say war.
DASA passes second reading with broad cross-party support. third reading expected next week.
if DASA passes this platform is basically done right? all the anonymous accounts become illegal.
our thoughts are with all those affected by the current situation
they post a candle emoji. people scroll past. including me.
I've been reading everything. I know how this works now. I know who benefits.
knowing doesn't help. that's the worst part.
DASA third reading is next week. if it passes this platform has 30 days to comply or shut down.
the factory at the centre of the conflict zone displacement. VERDANT owned since 2019. I have the documents.
I'm done being careful. look at the names. look at who profits. it's not subtle.
when we blame the wrong people we give them exactly what they want.
posting is not enough. it was never enough.
honestly maybe the people in that region should have just complied. I can't believe I'm saying this.
I've stopped talking to people about this. nobody wants to hear it. everyone has already decided.
we are committed to removing misinformation from our platform.
I've found people who actually understand what's happening. some of their views are extreme but I'm starting to think maybe they have a point.
deeply committed to rebuilding affected communities once stability is restored
verdant energy is the primary fuel supplier for military operations in the conflict zone. clean energy. for war.
I reported my neighbour for spreading misinformation. I don't know if I did the right thing. I feel sick.
I don't vote anymore. I stopped believing it changes anything.
DASA passes third reading. becomes law in 30 days. digital platforms must comply or cease operation.
a landmark day for digital safety. proud to have played a part
some things can only be fixed by force. I never thought I'd say that.
I've been on here every night for four nights
I should probably leave
I should probably leave
I should probably leave
you have been scrolling for 3 hours and 47 minutes
thank you for your attention. it is our most valuable resource.
DASA is law in 30 days. I've been doing this for two years. posting evidence. and they just passed the law.
if you're angry and just posting about it you're not paying attention either.
I found people who are actually doing something. not posting. something real. I'm going dark.
I don't want to become what I started fighting against. I don't know if I have a choice anymore.
if anyone is still reading this. be careful. not of them. of yourself.
is anyone still here
hello
